page: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps along with one complimentary public IPv4 address. The virtualization layer is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong overall performance, particularly in memory-intensive operations. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read-only performance at 4,744.5 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 279.53 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Compute performance is also robust, with both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests scoring in the top 25%. In database and key-value store benchmarks, the server demonstrated strong capabilities, executing 75.95 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and scoring in the top 25% for PassMark database operations.

Given its high memory capacity and strong memory bandwidth, the r3-256 is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, and data-intensive processing tasks. It also demonstrates strong performance in specific AI tasks, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds of 3,728.24 tokens per second with a small 135M parameter LLM. However, for larger models like the 7B parameter LLM, performance drops to average levels, and there is no data available for 70B parameter models. Additionally, while memory and database operations are highly efficient, static web serving throughput and single-threaded gzip compression remain in the average tier, suggesting that the instance is optimized more for backend data processing than simple web serving.
description: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.3 GHz, 256.0 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and strong read/write memory bandwidth. Compute and database capabilities are also strong, as evidenced by high Redis SET operations and stress-ng scores. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and data processing workloads, though it exhibits average performance in static web serving and larger LLM inference tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance for databases and data-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory bandwidth, and local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.

256.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory performance and high-speed Redis operations
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and data processing
